2606.26080.title: Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents
2606.26080.paper_summary: Process reward models enable fine-grained, step-level evaluation of LLMs, yet building them for agentic settings remains prohibitively difficult: long-horizon interactions, irreversible actions, and stochastic environment feedback make both human annotation and Monte Carlo estimation infeasible at scale. In this work, we show that reinforcement learning (RL) post-training already provides the ingredients for effective step-level scoring, eliminating the need for dedicated reward model training altogether. Concretely, we derive an implicit advantage under a general stochastic Markov decision process, which we term progress advantage -- log-probability ratio between the RL-trained policy and its reference policy exactly recovers the optimal advantage function. This formulation makes the resulting signal annotation-free, domain-agnostic, and available as a byproduct of the standard RL post-training pipeline. We validate the effectiveness of the progress advantage across three different applications: test-time scaling, uncertainty quantification, and failure attribution on five benchmarks and four model families. Across all settings, it consistently outperforms confidence-based baselines and, despite requiring no task-specific training, surpasses dedicated trained reward models. We complement these results with deeper analyses on characteristics of progress advantage, offering practical guidance for adoption in real-world agentic systems.
2606.26080.paper_summary_zh: 過程獎勵模型使得對大型語言模型（LLMs）進行細緻的逐步評估成為可能，但在代理環境中構建它們仍然極其困難：長期互動、不可逆的行動和隨機環境反饋使得在大規模上進行人類標註和蒙特卡羅估計變得不可行。在這項工作中，我們展示了增強學習（RL）後訓練已經提供了有效逐步評分的必要要素，完全消除了專門的獎勵模型訓練的需求。具體而言，我們在一個一般隨機馬爾可夫決策過程下推導出一個隱式優勢，我們稱之為進展優勢——增強學習訓練的策略與其參考策略之間的對數概率比恰好恢復了最佳優勢函數。這一公式使得所得到的信號無需標註、與領域無關，並作為標準增強學習後訓練流程的副產品可用。我們在三個不同的應用中驗證了進展優勢的有效性：測試時擴展、不確定性量化和五個基準和四個模型家族上的失敗歸因。在所有設定中，它始終超越基於信心的基準，儘管不需要特定任務的訓練，但仍超過了專門訓練的獎勵模型。我們通過對進展優勢特徵的深入分析來補充這些結果，為在現實世界代理系統中的採用提供實用指導。
2606.26080.author: Changdae Oh et.al.
2606.26080.authors: Changdae Oh, Wendi Li, Seongheon Park, Samuel Yeh, Tanwi Mallick, Sharon Li
